summary: Probed 4 alternate CC0 GLAM sources with the promisor-clone technique: Smithsonian (data moved to egress-blocked S3 — dead), MoMA (LFS pointers — dead), cmoa (repo deleted), Tate (69,202 artwork JSONs as PLAIN blobs, CC0 — acquisition works). Pivoted data source Met→Tate in DECISIONS.md. Built experiments/tate_convert.py and generated experiments/tate_sample.jsonl (290 real records via sparse-checkout of 4 directories at commit a51d8af). Verified met_tail.py unchanged against real data: tail/share/tags/--rare/show + date filters all pass.
third_party_note: 290 Tate CC0 metadata records, converted form, source+SHA recorded in THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md Item 2.
environment_note: S3 (smithsonian-open-access...amazonaws.com) egress-blocked like *.githubusercontent.com; github.com remains the only bulk-data route. Sparse-checkout on a blob:none clone batch-fetches blobs efficiently.
next: Run 7: scale the Tate sample (artwork_data.csv single 24MB plain blob, or more directories); re-evaluate tags/share usefulness at scale; consider renaming met_tail to a source-neutral name.
